phase1.title: Audit all interactive elements for data-testid coverage
Add data-testid to TopNavBar, QuantitiesPanel, ScalePanel, ClassificationGroups
Verify SF labels always visible on every polygon type
Canvas must accept standard PointerEvents for drawing
Document canvas coordinate contract for agent programming
CDP interaction test suite — verify all draw tools

phase2.title: Auto-naming sheets from title blocks on upload
Pattern Search — find repeated symbols across pages
Drawing Comparison — compare revisions, quantify changes
Merge tool — fill gaps between polygons
Split tool — divide polygon in two
Cut/subtract tool — remove area from polygon
Door/window backout from wall linear measurements

phase3.title: Assemblies — link classifications to materials + costs

bugs.description: save-to-org-btn used raw 'mx-org-library' localStorage key; ClassificationLibrary reads from 'mx-org-classifications'. Saved items never appeared in the org library panel.
Assembly cost estimate cast as { unitCost?: number } but Assembly type has no unitCost field — always produced $0.00. Fixed to compute cost from materials[].unitCost * (1/coverageRate).

cursor:update SSE event fell into default case — not routed to activity bus. useCollaboration hook never received cursor positions from other users. Fixed by adding cursor:update to the SSE event switch.

Collaborator cursors read via useStore.getState() (non-reactive) — cursors would never update in the render without a forced re-render trigger. Fixed by adding reactive useStore selector for collaborators at component top-level.

CustomFormulas component (P3-03) was built but never imported or wired into QuantitiesPanel. Users had no way to access custom formula editor. Fixed by: adding import, formulaModalClassification state, edit-row button with preview, modal render in all layouts, formula field preservation in saveEditing.

ClassificationUpdateSchema only included name/type/color/visible. All extended fields (formula, formulaUnit, formulaSavedToLibrary, tradeGroup, tileWidth, tileHeight, tileUnit, slopeFactor, breakdowns, customProperties, backouts, deductions) were silently stripped by Zod on every PATCH/PUT to /api/projects/[id]/classifications/[cid]. Custom formula saves via P3-03 modal were never persisted.

handleFullExport was client-side ExcelJS with only a flat quantities sheet — assemblies, estimates, and custom properties were never included in the download. Fixed by delegating to server-side /api/projects/[id]/export/excel route which generates all 5 sheets (Summary, Quantities, Assemblies, Estimates, Custom Properties).

deletePolygon ops in batch route called getPolygons(id) inside the loop — O(n) file reads for n deletePolygon ops (up to 500 ops = 500 file reads). Fixed by pre-loading all polygon IDs before the loop and using a Set for O(1) membership checks. Also update set on createPolygon/deletePolygon to keep consistent within same batch.

EstimatesTab built assemblyByType keyed by quantityFormula ("area"/"linear"/"count") — all classifications of the same type shared one assembly's unit cost, ignoring per-classification assembly assignments. Fixed by matching on classificationId first, falling back to formula/type match for unlinked classifications.

Custom formulas (P3-03) were stored in classification.formula but never applied to estimateQuantities. Formula result was displayed in the modal preview only — it never overrode the raw area/linear/count used in EstimatesTab cost calculations. Fixed by: (1) extracting tokenizer/evaluator to shared src/lib/formula-eval.ts, (2) applying applyCustomFormula() in estimateQuantities useMemo before falling back to raw geometry values.

AssembliesPanel used single project-level ppu for all pages — multi-page projects with per-page scales computed wrong quantities in Assemblies tab. Fixed to use scales[polygon.pageNumber] with fallback (mirrors QuantitiesPanel pattern).

GET and POST /api/estimates used single project-level ppu (getScale page-1 only) for all polygons across all pages. Multi-page projects computed wrong quantities server-side. Same root cause as BUG-PIKE-011 (AssembliesPanel). Fixed by importing listScales() and building per-page scale map with page-1 fallback — mirrors excel export route pattern.
/api/quantities used single page-1 ppu for all polygons — multi-page projects had incorrect quantity calculations on API/export side

Server-side quantities API and Excel export did not apply classification.formula overrides — client QuantitiesPanel showed formula results but API/export returned raw area/linear/count, creating data mismatch

CustomFormulas formula preview used single page-1 scale for all pages (multi-page inaccuracy) AND treated p.linearFeet as real-world units but it is raw pixels (linear_pixels in DB) — formula preview showed wildly inflated linear values. Fixed: use per-page scales from store.scales map and divide p.linearFeet by ppu.
AI takeoff summary used single global ppu for all pages + poly.linearFeet (raw pixels) instead of recalculating linear from points. Multi-page projects showed wrong LF totals.
linearFeet stored as real-world units in ai-results-loader (pre-divided by ppu) and in updateClassification type-change recalc — inconsistent with all other code paths that store raw pixel length
Share export (excel/pdf) used single global scale instead of per-page scales — multi-page projects would export wrong quantities

DrawingTool stored linearFeet as real-world feet (divided by ppu) — inconsistent with ai-results-loader (BUG-PIKE-017 fix) and updateClassification which store raw pixel length. CustomFormulas.tsx line 48 treats p.linearFeet as raw pixels (divides by ppu again), causing 2x error for DrawingTool-drawn polygons. Fixed: DrawingTool now passes ppu=1 to calculateLinearFeet — stores raw pixel length consistently.

Multiple display components summed/displayed polygon.linearFeet (raw pixels since BUG-PIKE-019 fix) without dividing by ppu. Affected: Wall Centerline total LF in SmartTools, all-page quantity sums in MXChat buildContext, classification group totals in ClassificationGroups, per-polygon and group stats in PolygonGroupPanel, and polygon detail display in PolygonProperties.
JSON export omitted assemblies — getAssemblies not fetched, assemblies not included in payload. Cost data was lost on export.
PolygonGroupPanel group summary stats (totalArea, totalLength) used global pixelsPerUnit instead of per-page PPU. Multi-page projects showed incorrect group totals.

GET and POST /api/estimates computed raw area/linear/count but never applied classification.formula overrides. /api/quantities and Excel export did apply formulas — estimates route was the odd one out, causing EstimatesTab cost calculations to ignore custom formulas set by users.
Share export (excel/pdf) buildQuantityRows did not apply classification.formula overrides — shared contractor reports showed raw geometry quantities instead of formula-adjusted values.
Contractor export buildQuantityRows did not apply classification.formula overrides — contractor HTML report showed raw geometry quantities instead of formula-adjusted values.

Contractor export and share export applied formula quantity overrides (BUG-025/024 fixes) but always used unitLabel(cls.type) as the unit, ignoring classification.formulaUnit. Excel export correctly used formulaUnit — unit mismatch across export formats.

measurex-api getTotals() used single global s.scale.pixelsPerUnit for all polygons across all pages. Multi-page projects reported incorrect totals in the agent automation API because different pages may have different scales.

Formula override not applied to count-type classifications in quantities API and estimates GET handler. count stayed as raw polygon count even when classification.formula was set. quantities route returned stale raw count; estimates GET built effectiveArea/effectiveLinear for area/linear only — count type was never overridden.

Org library save/import silently dropped formula, formulaUnit, tileWidth, tileHeight, and slopeFactor. OrgLibraryItem type missing formulaUnit; save-to-org-btn omitted formulaUnit; both import paths (bulk + double-click) only passed name/type/color/visible to addClassification. Round-tripping a formula classification through the org library lost all extended config.
count-type classification row displayed raw polygon count even when custom formula override was set — should show formula result + formulaUnit
Copy to clipboard button ignored custom formula overrides for all types — clipboard output was inconsistent with API exports
duplicate + restore routes did not copy tileWidth, tileHeight, tileUnit, slopeFactor from source classifications — these fields were silently dropped on project copy/restore
duplicate + restore routes drop all assemblies — cost engine data lost on project copy/import
/snapshot endpoint did not include assemblies in response — cost engine data was missing from agent/debug/round-trip dumps
Share JSON export omits assemblies — cost engine data (assemblies) missing from share link JSON download
Share Excel export missing Assemblies sheet — private Excel export has it but share export never fetched or rendered assemblies

DrawingTool had two identical data-testid="snapping-toggle" elements — one in area branch (non-count, non-linear) and one in linear branch. Duplicate testids cause agent snapshot ambiguity. Renamed to draw-snapping-toggle (area) and draw-linear-snapping-toggle (linear).

LeftToolbar wall-centerline toggle used old testid wall-centerline-toggle — DrawingTool was renamed to draw-wall-centerline-toggle in a prior commit but LeftToolbar was missed, creating testid fragmentation. Fixed by aligning to draw-wall-centerline-toggle.

CSV export produced raw polygon coordinates (polygon_id, x, y per point) — useless for estimators. Should produce quantities table matching Excel export (Classification, Type, Quantity, Unit). Fixed handleCsvCoordinatesExport to iterate previewRows (formula-aware) and output CSV quantities. Button label updated to CSV (quantities).

Markdown export polygon list showed Area: 0 for every linear and count polygon (always used poly.area / ppu²). Summary header "Total Area" summed area+linear+count totals together into one meaningless number. Fixed: polygon list now shows correct measurement+unit per type (SF/LF/EA); summary shows separate area/linear/count totals only when non-zero.

computeClassificationTotals (ExportPanel) never applied classification.formula overrides — screen-view Excel export, CSV export, and Markdown report classification table all showed raw geometry values instead of formula-adjusted quantities. Fixed by building rawByName map and calling applyCustomFormula() before computing totalValue and unit, mirroring QuantitiesPanel and all server-side routes.

GET + POST /api/estimates computed linearFeet totals without applying backout deductions (explicit door/window entries cls.backouts[], manual deductions cls.deductions[], auto-deductions from geometry-engine). Estimates were inconsistent with /api/quantities which DID apply deductions. Cost calculations used gross LF instead of net LF for any project with door/window backouts.

computeClassificationTotals in ExportPanel did not apply backout/deduction passes to totalLinear or rawByName before formula evaluation. Screen-view Excel, CSV quantities, and Markdown exports showed undeducted linear footage — inconsistent with QuantitiesPanel display and all server-side routes. Fixed by importing computeDeductions + aggregateDeductions and applying same deduction pass as quantities route.

EstimateSummary quantitiesByClass used single global ppu (page-1 only) for all polygons, never applied backout/auto/manual deductions to linearFeet, never applied custom formula overrides. The sidebar cost summary was inconsistent with QuantitiesPanel for multi-page projects, projects with door/window backouts, or classifications with custom formulas. Fixed: per-page scale lookup, computeDeductions + aggregateDeductions auto-deductions, cls.backouts + cls.deductions passes, applyCustomFormula override on second pass.

BUG-PIKE-042b: /api/estimates GET+POST applied formula overrides but not backout/deduction pass — LF inconsistent with /api/quantities. Fixed: added computeDeductions + aggregateDeductions + backouts + manualDeductions pass to both handlers before rawByName build.

BUG-PIKE-043: ExportPanel computeClassificationTotals missing deduction pass — screen-view Excel/CSV/Markdown showed gross LF not net LF. Fixed: imported computeDeductions + aggregateDeductions, applied deduction pass in rawByName build and totalLinear accumulation.
Both routes now consistently apply: cls.backouts[] + autoDeductMap (geometry-engine) + cls.deductions[] — all three deduction types, matching QuantitiesPanel.

All 43 bugs confirmed fixed in source. Full deduction coverage now: QuantitiesPanel, /api/quantities, /api/estimates GET+POST, /api/export/excel, /api/export/contractor, /api/share/export, ExportPanel client preview.
Regression sweep: 2 files changed, 59 insertions — clean diff, no unintended changes
BUG-PIKE-044: EstimateSummary sidebar cost summary used single-page ppu, no deductions, no formula overrides — fixed with per-page scales + full deduction pass + formula override
